title: WCAG 2.2 AA hardening + manual assistive-tech audit
context: oer-science-labs is designed for educators in low-resource settings; accessibility is a hard requirement, not a nice-to-have. The browse UI (code-008) must be hardened to WCAG 2.2 AA through both automated checks and a manual assistive-technology audit across the defined support matrix. Printable output must also be accessible and readable. This task closes the M1 milestone's accessibility requirement.
objective: Harden the oer-science-labs UI (browse, guide-detail, and print views) to WCAG 2.2 AA through automated axe/pa11y checks and a documented manual assistive-technology audit across the full support matrix: NVDA+Firefox/Windows, JAWS+Chrome/Windows, VoiceOver+Safari/macOS and iOS, TalkBack+Chrome/Android, and keyboard-only on each desktop browser. Fix all identified critical and high-severity issues. Produce a signed-off audit report.
acceptanceCriteria: Automated axe/pa11y checks pass with 0 critical issues across browse, guide-detail, and print views — gated in CI

Manual assistive-technology audit is documented and completed across: NVDA+Firefox/Windows, JAWS+Chrome/Windows, VoiceOver+Safari/macOS, VoiceOver+Safari/iOS, TalkBack+Chrome/Android, and keyboard-only on each supported desktop browser
All critical and high-severity issues found in the manual audit are fixed before merge
Printable output is readable and navigable with screen readers; print CSS does not produce inaccessible artifacts
Offline functionality is preserved after a11y changes; offline E2E still passes
Manual audit is signed off by a qualified accessibility reviewer and the sign-off is recorded in the PR
No telemetry or new network requests introduced; CSP and no-telemetry CI gates continue to pass
output: A pull request hardening the oer-science-labs UI to WCAG 2.2 AA with automated axe/pa11y gates and a signed-off manual assistive-technology audit report.
